title: Aureline manual emergency-import receipt contract
description: Boundary schema for manual_import_receipt_record, the reviewable artifact produced when an operator imports signed emergency metadata (emergency_action_record or revocation_record) into a mirrored, offline, or air-gapped target that cannot rely on a live authoritative pull. One receipt records who imported the metadata, the source artifact and import path, the detached-signature verification outcome, the observed signer continuity, the apply time, the target scope, the freshness and supersedence state that now applies, the follow-up obligations the import imposes, and a metadata-chain stub linking the receipt to its authoritative-origin predecessor and to any downstream receipts it supersedes. Raw bundle bytes, raw signatures, raw trust-root payloads, raw operator identifiers, raw device identifiers, and raw file bodies never cross this boundary; the record carries opaque refs, typed vocabulary, and redaction-safe summary fields only. This receipt is the object that mirror-import views, manual-import surfaces, admin exports, and support packets quote when they say 'this emergency action was imported here'; surfaces MUST NOT invent receipt-local freshness, signer-continuity, or supersedence terms that disagree with the parent emergency_action_record or revocation_record. Adding a new operator_class, import_path_class, verification_outcome_class, applied_state_class, target_scope_class, import_supersedence_state_class, receipt_state_class, metadata_chain_link_class, or follow_up_obligation_class is additive-minor and requires a manual_import_receipt_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row co-signed by security_trust_review and release_council.

manual_import_receipt_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this manual-import receipt export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator. Every receipt carries record_kind = manual_import_receipt_record so parity audits, admin exports, and support packets can validate payloads mechanically.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / admin exports / support packets. Raw bundle bytes, raw signatures, raw trust-root payloads, raw operator identifiers, and raw device identifiers never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short, reviewable sentence describing posture, continuity, verification, or operator guidance. Raw secret material and raw payload bytes MUST NOT appear.

imported_record_kind_class.description: Which record kind from schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json this receipt imported. Mirrors the discriminator on that schema so a receipt and its parent record cannot drift on kind.

operator_class.description: Frozen operator-class vocabulary. Mirrors schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json#/$defs/required_action_actor_class plus security_trust_review for receipts minted during private triage or post-incident review.

operator_account_ref.description: Opaque ref to the operator account or service principal the receipt attributes the import to. Raw operator identifiers (email, login handle, real name, machine hostname) MUST NOT appear.

operator_role_ref.description: Opaque ref to the operator role granting authority for this import. Null when the operator_class alone carries the authority (for example a local workstation admin).

operator_identity.description: Operator identity envelope. Names who imported the emergency metadata under stable opaque refs so mirror/manual-import views, admin exports, and support packets agree on attribution without leaking raw identifiers.

import_path_class.description: Frozen import-path vocabulary. file_import is a signed metadata file imported via a managed UI or CLI. offline_transfer and removable_media_transfer cover manual snapshot movement. approved_courier_transfer and cross_domain_transfer cover escorted transfers across trust boundaries. air_gap_relay_transfer covers a staged relay between air-gapped environments.

source_artifact_class.description: Frozen source-artifact vocabulary naming the shape of the imported file. The receipt does not embed the file; it records what was imported.

source_artifact_ref.description: Opaque ref to the source-artifact manifest or export record the receipt imported. Raw file paths, raw URLs, raw media serial numbers, and raw courier identifiers MUST NOT appear.

upstream_distribution_id_ref.description: Opaque ref into the parent emergency_action_record or revocation_record distribution_statuses[].distribution_id the receipt instantiates on this target. Null only when the authoritative distribution row is not yet minted; the receipt is non-publishable in that state.

upstream_receipt_ref.description: Opaque ref to an upstream manual-import receipt (for example a parent mirror that this target imported from). Null when the receipt chains directly from the authoritative origin.

source_artifact_record.description: Source-artifact envelope. Records what was imported and where it chained from under opaque refs so supersedence and metadata-chain links are auditable without leaking raw filesystem paths or transport identifiers.

detached_signature_status_class.description: Frozen detached-signature-presence vocabulary. distinct from outcome: the receipt records whether a signature was present at all before recording whether it verified.

verification_outcome_class.description: Frozen verification-outcome vocabulary. Covers the happy path (same signer, rotated with continuity statement, cross-signed), typed failure cases (mismatch, revoked, expired, unknown), explicit policy-admitted skip (used only where break-glass policy admits it), policy-rejected skip (never admissible), and pending-review (human reviewer required before the receipt becomes active).

verifying_tool_ref.description: Opaque ref to the verifying-tool manifest (offline verifier build or managed client). Null only when verification_outcome = verification_skipped_policy_admitted.

verification_policy_ref.description: Opaque ref to the policy or break-glass profile that authorized the verification posture. Required on every receipt so a policy-admitted skip is not a one-off decision.

verification_envelope.description: Detached-signature verification envelope. Records whether a signature was present, the typed verification outcome, the tool and policy that authorized the outcome, and the start / complete timestamps. Raw cryptographic material (signatures, fingerprints, trust-root bytes) never appear here.

signer_continuity_state_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json so a receipt and the imported record cannot disagree on signer continuity.

distribution_freshness_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/security/emergency_action_record.schema.json so a receipt and the imported record share one freshness vocabulary. A receipt MUST NOT claim authoritative_live; manual-import receipts record snapshot / mirror / offline / unknown freshness only.

target_scope_class.description: Frozen target-scope vocabulary. Names the breadth of the import. Mirror and offline-bundle scopes narrow into one mirror or one offline bundle; trust_root_scope covers a trust-root successor import that narrows subsequent signer verification.

applied_state_class.description: Frozen applied-state vocabulary. applied and applied_with_narrowed_scope are the two success postures. applied_and_overrode_prior_snapshot records that the import displaced an older snapshot on the same target. rejected_at_* records a typed rejection. pending_operator_confirmation is the intermediate state after verification but before the operator commits the import. rolled_back is a reviewable reversal.

import_supersedence_state_class.description: Frozen import-supersedence vocabulary. Resolves the relationship between the receipt and any prior snapshot already installed on the target, plus a forward-looking value for receipts that themselves have been superseded by a later import.

receipt_state_class.description: Frozen receipt-lifecycle vocabulary. draft is pre-apply. active is the imported-and-applied posture. superseded, expired, withdrawn, and reconciled are durable end states.

install_profile_card_refs.description: Opaque refs into artifacts/release/install_topology_matrix.yaml. Each ref names one install_profile_card_record the import applies to. Empty is admissible only when target_scope_class in {mirror_snapshot, offline_bundle_scope, trust_root_scope} and the mirror/offline/trust-root refs name the scope instead.

deployment_profile_scope.description: Deployment-profile scope the import narrows. MUST be a subset of the imported record's deployment_profile_scope.

mirror_scope_refs.description: Opaque refs to the mirror snapshots the import writes into. Empty when target_scope_class is not a mirror.

offline_bundle_scope_refs.description: Opaque refs to the offline bundles the import writes into. Empty when target_scope_class is not an offline bundle.

target_scope_record.description: Target-scope envelope. Records the breadth and identity of the environment the import lands in so later surfaces can reason about where the receipt is authoritative without re-deriving the scope.

expiry_hook_class.description: Frozen expiry-hook vocabulary. Default is inherit_parent_record_expiry so a receipt does not mint a looser lifetime than the imported record. receipt_local_expiry_override is admissible only when the receipt narrows the expiry (makes it earlier) and cites a policy_ref.

effective_expiry_at.description: The absolute expiry the target should honor. Null admissible only when expiry_hook_class = no_expiry_pending_reconciliation.

expiry_envelope.description: Expiry envelope. Records how the receipt derives its effective expiry and the policy that authorized any local override.

follow_up_obligation_class.description: Frozen follow-up-obligation vocabulary. One receipt may record several obligations; each row carries a typed class plus owner, deadline semantics, and deadline. Surfaces MUST render obligations as typed rows rather than flattening them to one generic to-do.

follow_up_obligation_record.description: One typed follow-up obligation row. Mirrors the emergency_action_record.required_action vocabulary for deadline_semantics so a receipt and its parent record resolve deadlines under one vocabulary.

metadata_chain_link_class.description: Frozen metadata-chain link vocabulary. Lets the receipt enumerate its predecessors, its supersedence links, and parallel sibling receipts so later tooling can rebuild the import chain without guessing.

peer_ref.description: Opaque ref to the peer receipt, authoritative distribution row, mirror snapshot, or offline snapshot this link connects to.

metadata_chain_link_record.description: One metadata-chain link row. The receipt's chain_links[] array assembles these into a reviewable graph of where the imported metadata came from and where it went.

chain_id.description: Opaque stable id for the metadata chain this receipt participates in. Stable across downstream receipts that chain from the same authoritative origin.

authoritative_origin_ref.description: Opaque ref to the authoritative emergency_action_record / revocation_record the chain ultimately roots in. Required so even a multi-hop air-gapped import can be traced back to its signed origin.

metadata_chain_stub.description: Metadata-chain stub. Locks the receipt into a single reviewable chain rooted at an authoritative origin record, plus typed links to upstream and downstream peers. This is the stub the handoff spec requires so a mirrored or air-gapped import leaves a reviewable trail rather than an untracked local state flip.

imported_emergency_action_ref.description: Opaque ref to the emergency_action_record this receipt imported. Null iff imported_revocation_ref is populated.

imported_revocation_ref.description: Opaque ref to the revocation_record this receipt imported. Null iff imported_emergency_action_ref is populated.

relationship_links.description: Typed relation envelope binding the receipt to exactly one imported emergency_action_record or revocation_record, to upstream and downstream peer receipts, and to the same advisory / incident / support / admin-export graph the parent record already uses.

title.description: Short, reviewable receipt title suitable for admin exports, support packets, and mirror/manual-import surfaces.

applied_at.description: When the receipt entered applied / applied_with_narrowed_scope / applied_and_overrode_prior_snapshot state. Null while applied_state is draft, pending_operator_confirmation, rejected_at_verification, rejected_at_policy, or rolled_back.

allOf.description: Exactly one of imported_emergency_action_ref or imported_revocation_ref is populated, matching imported_record_kind.
Manual-import receipts never claim authoritative_live freshness; live freshness is reserved for the authoritative origin distribution row.
A receipt that claims any applied posture must have a non-null applied_at.
A receipt whose verification_outcome is a typed failure class MUST NOT claim any applied posture.
A receipt in receipt_state = active MUST have applied_state in the applied family and applied_at populated.
A receipt in receipt_state = superseded MUST name the superseding receipt and MAY record import_itself_later_superseded in supersedence_state.

manual_import_receipt_record.description: Manual emergency-import receipt record. One receipt per manual / mirrored / offline / air-gapped import of an emergency_action_record or revocation_record into a named target. Binds operator, import path, source artifact, detached-signature verification, observed signer continuity, freshness and validation on this target, target scope, applied state, supersedence and expiry, follow-up obligations, and a metadata-chain stub into one reviewable object so mirror-import views, admin exports, and support packets preserve the same identity the parent emergency record carries.
